questions.question: A florist has 349 daffodils. She ties them into bunches of 8. How many full bunches can she make, and how many are left?
questions.explanation: 349 ÷ 8 = 43 remainder 5. 8 goes into 34 four times (32), remainder 2. Bring down 9 to make 29. 8 goes into 29 three times (24), remainder 5. Answer: 43 bunches, 5 left. Check: 8 × 43 + 5 = 349. ✓

questions.question: A delivery driver has 427 parcels to load equally into 9 vans. How many parcels go in each van, and how many are left?
questions.explanation: 427 ÷ 9 = 47 remainder 4. 9 goes into 42 four times (36), remainder 6. Bring down 7 to make 67. 9 goes into 67 seven times (63), remainder 4. Answer: 47 parcels, 4 left. Check: 9 × 47 + 4 = 427. ✓

questions.question: A warehouse has 578 tins of paint. They are packed into crates of 9. How many full crates are there, and how many tins are left?
questions.explanation: 578 ÷ 9 = 64 remainder 2. 9 goes into 57 six times (54), remainder 3. Bring down 8 to make 38. 9 goes into 38 four times (36), remainder 2. Answer: 64 crates, 2 left. Check: 9 × 64 + 2 = 578. ✓

questions.question: A school library has 643 bookmarks to share equally among 7 year groups. How many bookmarks does each year group get, and how many are left?
questions.explanation: 643 ÷ 7 = 91 remainder 6. 7 goes into 64 nine times (63), remainder 1. Bring down 3 to make 13. 7 goes into 13 once (7), remainder 6. Answer: 91 each, 6 left. Check: 7 × 91 + 6 = 643. ✓

questions.question: A chef has 815 cherry tomatoes. She puts 6 on each plate. How many plates can she prepare, and how many tomatoes are left?
questions.explanation: 815 ÷ 6 = 135 remainder 5. 6 goes into 8 once (6), remainder 2. 6 goes into 21 three times (18), remainder 3. 6 goes into 35 five times (30), remainder 5. Answer: 135 plates, 5 left. Check: 6 × 135 + 5 = 815. ✓

questions.question: A printer produces 947 leaflets. They are bundled into groups of 8. How many full bundles are made, and how many leaflets are left?
questions.explanation: 947 ÷ 8 = 118 remainder 3. 8 goes into 9 once (8), remainder 1. 8 goes into 14 once (8), remainder 6. 8 goes into 67 eight times (64), remainder 3. Answer: 118 bundles, 3 left. Check: 8 × 118 + 3 = 947. ✓

questions.question: A lift holds 8 people. There are 35 people waiting. How many trips must the lift make to carry everyone?
questions.explanation: 35 ÷ 8 = 4 remainder 3. Four trips carry 32 people, leaving 3 behind. A fifth trip is needed. So 5 trips. ✓

questions.question: A packet holds 6 muffins. Sam bakes 26 muffins. How many full packets can he fill?
questions.explanation: 26 ÷ 6 = 4 remainder 2. He can fill 4 full packets. The 2 leftover muffins are not enough for another full packet. ✓

questions.question: Tables seat 5 people each. There are 23 guests at a party. How many tables are needed so everyone has a seat?
questions.explanation: 23 ÷ 5 = 4 remainder 3. Four tables seat 20, leaving 3 without seats. A fifth table is needed. So 5 tables. ✓

questions.question: A jar holds 7 pencils. Tom has 45 pencils. How many full jars can he fill?
questions.explanation: 45 ÷ 7 = 6 remainder 3. He can fill 6 full jars. The 3 leftover pencils do not fill another jar. ✓

questions.question: Benches hold 4 children each. There are 19 children in the class. How many benches are needed?
questions.explanation: 19 ÷ 4 = 4 remainder 3. Four benches seat 16 children, leaving 3 without seats. A fifth bench is needed. So 5 benches. ✓

questions.question: A baker cuts a 29 cm ribbon into 8 cm pieces. How many complete pieces can she cut?
questions.explanation: 29 ÷ 8 = 3 remainder 5. She can cut 3 complete pieces. The remaining 5 cm is too short for another 8 cm piece. ✓

questions.question: A taxi holds 4 passengers. There are 27 people who need a taxi. How many taxis must be booked?
questions.explanation: 27 ÷ 4 = 6 remainder 3. Six taxis carry 24, leaving 3 people. A seventh taxi is needed. So 7 taxis. ✓

questions.question: Eggs come in boxes of 6. A recipe needs 32 eggs. How many boxes must be bought?
questions.explanation: 32 ÷ 6 = 5 remainder 2. Five boxes give only 30 eggs, not enough. A sixth box is needed (6 × 6 = 36). So 6 boxes. ✓

questions.question: A rope is 200 cm long. It is cut into pieces of 15 cm. How many complete pieces can be cut?
questions.explanation: 200 ÷ 15 = 13 remainder 5. He can cut 13 complete pieces. The remaining 5 cm is too short for another 15 cm piece. Check: 15 × 13 = 195, remainder 5. ✓

questions.question: A school trip needs to transport 83 children. Each minibus holds 14 children. How many minibuses must be booked?
questions.explanation: 83 ÷ 14 = 5 remainder 13. Five minibuses carry 70 children, leaving 13. A sixth minibus is needed. So 6 minibuses. ✓

questions.question: A gardener has 100 plants. Each row holds 7 plants. How many complete rows can she plant?
questions.explanation: 100 ÷ 7 = 14 remainder 2. She can plant 14 complete rows. The 2 leftover plants do not make a full row. Check: 7 × 14 = 98, remainder 2. ✓

questions.question: A baker needs 250 grams of butter. Butter comes in 90g packets. How many packets must she buy?
questions.explanation: 250 ÷ 90 = 2 remainder 70. Two packets give 180g, not enough. A third packet is needed (3 × 90 = 270g). So 3 packets. ✓

questions.question: A hall has 150 chairs. They are set out in rows of 11. How many chairs are in the incomplete last row?
questions.explanation: 150 ÷ 11 = 13 remainder 7. There are 13 full rows and one partial row with 7 chairs. Check: 11 × 13 = 143, and 150 - 143 = 7. ✓

questions.question: An egg box holds 12 eggs. A farmer has 500 eggs. How many full boxes can he fill?
questions.explanation: 500 ÷ 12 = 41 remainder 8. He can fill 41 full boxes. The remaining 8 eggs do not fill another box. Check: 12 × 41 = 492, remainder 8. ✓

questions.question: A ferry carries 35 cars per trip. There are 150 cars waiting. How many ferry trips are needed?
questions.explanation: 150 ÷ 35 = 4 remainder 10. Four trips carry 140 cars, leaving 10. A fifth trip is needed. So 5 trips. ✓

questions.question: Children sit in groups of 6 for lunch. There are 100 children. How many full tables are there?
questions.explanation: 100 ÷ 6 = 16 remainder 4. There are 16 full tables. The remaining 4 children sit at a partially filled table. ✓

questions.question: A charity posts letters costing 85p each. They have £20 to spend. How many letters can they post?
questions.explanation: £20 = 2000p. 2000 ÷ 85 = 23 remainder 45. They can post 23 letters (23 × 85 = 1955p). The remaining 45p is not enough for another letter. ✓

questions.question: A lift has a weight limit of 400 kg. Each person weighs approximately 65 kg. What is the maximum number of people the lift can carry at once?
questions.explanation: 400 ÷ 65 = 6 remainder 10. Six people weigh 390 kg, which is within the limit. Seven people would weigh 455 kg, exceeding it. So maximum 6 people. ✓

questions.question: A school orders pencils in packs of 24. They need at least 500 pencils. How many packs must they order?
questions.explanation: 500 ÷ 24 = 20 remainder 20. Twenty packs give only 480, not enough. Twenty-one packs give 504, which is enough. So 21 packs. ✓

questions.question: A lorry can carry 750 kg. Boxes weigh 32 kg each. How many boxes can the lorry carry?
questions.explanation: 750 ÷ 32 = 23 remainder 14. Twenty-three boxes weigh 736 kg (within the limit). Twenty-four boxes would weigh 768 kg (over the limit). So 23 boxes. ✓

questions.question: Tiles are 15 cm wide. A wall is 200 cm wide. How many whole tiles fit across the wall, and how wide is the gap left?
questions.explanation: 200 ÷ 15 = 13 remainder 5. Thirteen tiles cover 195 cm, leaving a 5 cm gap. Check: 15 × 13 = 195, and 200 - 195 = 5. ✓

questions.question: A concert hall has 1,000 seats. Each row has 38 seats. How many full rows are there, and how many seats are in the last row?
questions.explanation: 1000 ÷ 38 = 26 remainder 12. There are 26 full rows of 38 seats, and the last row has 12 seats. Check: 38 × 26 = 988, and 1000 - 988 = 12. ✓

questions.question: Which is the closest estimate for 189 ÷ 4?
questions.explanation: Round 189 to 200. 200 ÷ 4 = 50. The actual answer is 47.25, so 50 is the closest estimate. ✓

questions.question: Harry says 132 ÷ 11 = 14. He checks by multiplying 14 × 11. What answer does he get?
questions.explanation: To check, multiply: 14 × 11 = 154 ≠ 132. So Harry is wrong. The correct answer is 12 (11 × 12 = 132). ✓

questions.question: Estimate 297 ÷ 6 by rounding 297 to the nearest hundred.
questions.explanation: Round 297 to 300. 300 ÷ 6 = 50. The actual answer is 49.5, so 50 is a good estimate. ✓

questions.question: A pupil calculates 864 ÷ 36 = 24. Use multiplication to check. Is she correct?
questions.explanation: Check: 36 × 24 = 864. This matches, so she is correct. ✓

questions.question: Estimate 612 ÷ 29 by rounding both numbers.
questions.explanation: Round 612 to 600 and 29 to 30. 600 ÷ 30 = 20. The actual answer is approximately 21.1, so 20 is a reasonable estimate. ✓

questions.question: Tom estimates 378 ÷ 9 by rounding 378 to 360. What estimate does he get?
questions.explanation: 360 ÷ 9 = 40. Tom's estimate is 40. The actual answer is 42, so the estimate is reasonable. ✓

questions.question: Freya says 1,120 ÷ 35 = 34. Without doing the full calculation, is this reasonable?
options: No, the answer should be around 32
No, the answer should be around 30
No, the answer should be around 36
No, the answer should be much bigger
questions.correctAnswer: No, the answer should be around 32
questions.explanation: Estimate: 1,120 is close to 1,050. 1,050 ÷ 35 = 30. Try 35 × 34 = 1,190 ≠ 1,120. Try 35 × 32 = 1,120. The correct answer is 32. ✓

questions.question: Without calculating exactly, which is the best estimate for 3,648 ÷ 72?
questions.explanation: Round 3,648 to 3,500 and 72 to 70. 3,500 ÷ 70 = 50. The actual answer is approximately 50.7, so 50 is an excellent estimate. ✓

questions.question: Jake calculates 2,484 ÷ 54 = 46. Mia calculates 2,484 ÷ 54 = 48. Who is correct? Check using multiplication.
questions.explanation: Check Jake: 54 × 46 = 2,484. This matches exactly, so Jake is correct. Check Mia: 54 × 48 = 2,592 ≠ 2,484. ✓

questions.question: Estimate 7,956 ÷ 82. Which answer is closest?
questions.explanation: Round 7,956 to 8,000 and 82 to 80. 8,000 ÷ 80 = 100. The actual answer is 97 (82 × 97 = 7,954). So 97 is the closest. ✓

questions.question: A PE teacher has 84 tennis balls to share equally among 7 groups. How many balls does each group get?
questions.explanation: Using the bus stop method: 7 goes into 8 once (7), remainder 1. Bring down 4 to make 14. 7 goes into 14 twice (14). Answer: 12. Check: 7 × 12 = 84. ✓

questions.question: A factory packs 396 biscuits into packets of 11. How many packets are made?
questions.explanation: Using the bus stop method: 11 goes into 39 three times (33), remainder 6. Bring down 6 to make 66. 11 goes into 66 six times (66). Answer: 36. Check: 11 × 36 = 396. ✓
